title: Auth bootstrap dedup — share AuthProvider.refresh() in-flight promise to prevent tab-duplicate logout
scope: One-line fix in app/contexts/AuthContext.tsx: collapse concurrent refresh() callers onto a single in-flight Promise<void> via useRef, so React StrictMode dev double-mount and duplicated browser tabs don't both POST /api/auth/refresh with the same one-time-use rt cookie.
value: Duplicating a Vector browser tab redirected the user to /login. Root cause: the rt HttpOnly refresh-token cookie is origin-scoped, so it gets copied into the duplicate tab. Both tabs mount fresh, both fire AuthProvider's bootstrap effect, and both POST /api/auth/refresh with the same token. Backend service.go Refresh-token-reuse detection (UPDATE sessions SET revoked = TRUE WHERE user_id = $1) treats the second request as a stolen-token attack and revokes every session for the user. Same race fires under React 18 StrictMode dev double-mount. Sharing one in-flight promise removes the race entirely without weakening the backend's reuse detection.
implementation_plan: ✓ Add useRef import + refreshInFlight ref in AuthProvider
✓ Wrap refresh() body so concurrent callers return the same Promise<void>
✓ Reset the ref in finally() when the flight resolves/rejects
feature_list: AuthProvider.refresh() shared in-flight promise — concurrent callers reuse the same fetch
work_item_backlog: 00457 — Auth bootstrap dedup — share AuthProvider.refresh() in-flight promise (FE-SEC-0012, EST-F2, RISK-LOW)
acceptance_criteria: Duplicate a logged-in Vector browser tab → both tabs stay signed in (no /login redirect)
React StrictMode dev mount fires bootstrap effect twice but only one POST /api/auth/refresh goes out
Existing 401-retry path in app/lib/api.ts (which has its own _refreshPromise) is unaffected
AuthContext unit tests still pass; no regression to login/logout flows
risks: RISK-LOW: useRef-based dedup is a well-understood React idiom; failure mode is at worst the original race re-emerging, never silent data loss
references: backend/internal/auth/service.go — Refresh-token-reuse branch (lines 179-184) that revokes all sessions
